title: Sentiment Tilt Meter
subtitle: Composite Candle Sentiment Score: CBR + CPP + VAD with Multi-TF Fusion and Exponential Smoothing
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: HIGH EXTRACT — CBR/CPP/VAD sentiment score formula is an entirely new signal type. Composite micro-score aggregation, exponential smoothing, flip threshold. Tested on Crash 300/1000. Different from VWV — captures candle geometry sentiment, not velocity.

core_concept.name: Sentiment Tilt Meter
core_concept.description: Each candle receives a micro-score from 3 geometric features. Scores are averaged over a lookback window, fused across timeframes (weighted), then exponentially smoothed. Output is a -100 to +100 sentiment score. Flip of ≥60 points = signal.

three_features.CBR: Candle Body Ratio — body size relative to total range, sign from direction. `cbr = (body/range) * (+1 if bullish else -1)`
three_features.CPP: Close Position Percent — where close sits in the high-low range. `cpp = ((close-low)/(high-low) - 0.5) * 2` → -1 to +1
three_features.VAD: Volatility-Adjusted Distance — range vs ATR. `vad = ((range/atr) - 1.0) * (sign of close vs open)`

confidence_factors.prior_bar_confirm: × 1.0 if prior bar direction matches, × 0.6 otherwise

core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: STM provides a sentiment score that complements our binary gates. Adding STM as a continuous gate (score > 30 for BUY) allows partial satisfaction. CBR and CPP are new features not in our VWV. CPP alone is a useful entry timing feature.

formulas.CBR: (abs(close - open) / (high - low)) * (1 if close > open else -1)
formulas.VAD: ((high - low) / atr - 1.0) * (1 if close > open else -1)
formulas.confidence: mini * (1.0 if prev_bar_aligned else 0.6) * (0.6 if atr < quiet_threshold else 1.0)

InpFlipThreshold.description: Score change magnitude to trigger flip signal

keep: CBR (Candle Body Ratio): captures body size relative to range with direction
CPP (Close Position Percent): where close sits in the candle range — key for entry timing
VAD (Volatility-Adjusted Distance): body vs ATR with direction
Confidence reduction (0.6×) when prior bar doesn't confirm
discard: Flip threshold as signal trigger — use score level instead for our gate
improve: Use CPP alone for entry timing: CPP > 0.5 (closed in top half of range) for BUY departure = price closed strong
Use STM score as gate: require score > 30 for BUY signals on Crash/Boom
Replace VWV with STM for candle quality assessment: STM captures CBR + CPP + VAD vs our VWV single dimension

values.1.0: close at the high = strongest bullish bar
values.-1.0: close at the low = strongest bearish bar

cpp_for_entry_timing.gate_rule: BUY departure: require cpp > 0.3 (closed in top 65% of range). SELL: cpp < -0.3.
